events.note: Writing Intent Check (revision 1) for the Atlas → Globalping migration scope

events.change_summary: Intent Check: full Atlas → Globalping replacement for zero-config in-network measurements

events.summary: Intent Check (revision 1): replace RIPE Atlas with Globalping as the sole in-network measurement backend, removing the API key/credit requirement for zero-config operation. Release/publishing and local-install cleanup are out of scope.

events.note: Exploring Atlas integration surface, then writing full scope as revision 2

events.change_summary: Full scope: replace RIPE Atlas backend with Globalping across 2 phases — new keyless backend module with tests, then CLI/display/docs rewiring and Atlas deletion

events.rationale: Backend swap with no UI work — terminal label and README text changes only, so design is skipped. Architecture is skipped because the new Globalping module is a one-for-one replacement of atlas.py inside an existing, well-understood boundary (discover, schedule, poll, parse) and the scope already carries detailed per-phase Files and Verification. Standard tier: about 7 files, builds entirely on existing module and error-handling patterns.

events.decision: Keep country.py's keyless Atlas probe-IP lookup (_get_atlas_probe_ip / RIPE_ATLAS_PROBES) despite deleting the Atlas measurement backend.
events.rationale: That lookup serves local measurements only — it finds a routable IPv4 per ASN to use as a traceroute target from the user's machine — and requires no API key. Globalping publishes no probe IP addresses, so there is nothing to migrate it to. The acceptance-criteria grep for "atlas" therefore excludes country.py, and tests/test_country.py stays untouched.

events.decision: Use Globalping's mtr measurement type (not traceroute) for the inside-out path from probes back to the user.
events.rationale: Globalping mtr results carry a per-hop ASN field, so the new module's AS-path parser needs no Cymru bulk lookup round-trip — unlike the Atlas parser, which had to resolve every hop IP via cymru_bulk_lookup_rich. Fewer network dependencies, simpler pure-function parsing, and the exact hop field names get verified against a live response in Phase 1.

events.decision: Remote in-network measurements run by default in country mode, gated by a new --no-remote opt-out flag instead of an API key.
events.rationale: The Atlas backend was implicitly opt-in via --atlas-key; with the key requirement gone, Globalping would silently become always-on, adding sweep time and consuming shared per-IP rate limits. A --no-remote flag (styled after --no-throughput) preserves a cheap escape hatch without reintroducing any configuration requirement. An optional --gp-token / NETPATH_GLOBALPING_TOKEN (mirroring --cf-token) raises the rate-limit tier for heavy users.

events.decision: Rename the atlas-profile command to coverage, and determine sweep coverage from a single GET /v1/probes inventory call instead of per-ASN queries.
events.rationale: The command name must not reference the old service (per approved intent), and "coverage" describes what it shows without binding the CLI surface to a vendor name. The single probe-inventory fetch returns every connected probe with ASN and country metadata, so one request powers both the pre-sweep "probes found in X/Y ASNs" report and the per-country coverage table — replacing N per-ASN Atlas queries plus the paginated coverage crawl. The anchors column disappears; Globalping has no anchor class (accepted trade-off).

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2. Two phases: (1) build the new Globalping backend module with unit tests, (2) switch cli.py/display.py/globe.py/README over and delete atlas.py and its tests. 13 requirements, 9 acceptance criteria. Key calls: mtr measurement type gives per-hop ASN so the remote parser drops the Cymru lookup; remote measurements default-on with a --no-remote opt-out and optional --gp-token for higher rate limits; atlas-profile becomes coverage; country.py's keyless Atlas probe-IP oracle for local test-IP discovery stays (Globalping publishes no probe IPs). Workflow classified backend-feature/standard, skipping design and architecture. Four decisions logged.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting phase 1: building the Globalping backend module and its unit tests

events.description: Baseline clean (75 passed, ruff ok); Globalping schema verified against live spec; implementing globalping.py
Module and tests written; running verification checks
